model_narrative: We arrange the contents of a table that has a fixed grid of rows and columns onto a page of limited width. For each row we choose one height out of a list of candidate heights allowed for that row, and from those choices we determine the width given to each cell and to each column. The objective is to minimize the total height of the table.

sets.name: R
sets.doc: the rows of the table, indexed by integers

sets.name: C
sets.doc: the columns of the table, indexed by integers

sets.name: rh
sets.doc: the feasible row-height assignments, given as the set of pairs of a row together with a candidate height it is allowed to take; the second element of each pair is the actual numeric height of the row measured in width-equivalent units, and not every row offers the same list of candidate heights

params.name: pw
params.doc: the page width available for the whole layout, in width units; a single scalar

params.name: minRCHw
params.doc: the minimum width that a given cell requires when its row is set to a particular candidate height, in width units; indexed by a feasible row-height pair together with a column, so it is only defined for the candidate heights that row is allowed to take

vars.name: bRH
vars.doc: selection indicator over feasible row-height pairs; equals 1 if the row is set to that candidate height and 0 otherwise

vars.name: xRCw
vars.doc: the width assigned to each individual cell of the table

vars.name: xCw
vars.doc: the width assigned to each column of the table

vars.name: toth
vars.doc: the total height of the table, in height units; a single scalar

constraints.name: bRH[r,
constraints.intent: Each row of the table must be set to exactly one height. For every row, across all of the candidate heights that row is allowed to take, exactly one of them is selected.

constraints.name: xRCw[r,
constraints.intent: The width of each cell is determined by the height chosen for its row. For every cell, its width equals the minimum width that cell requires under whichever candidate height its row ends up taking.

constraints.name: xCw[c]
constraints.intent: Each column must be wide enough to hold every cell in that column. For every cell, the width of its column must be at least the width of that cell.

constraints.name: xCw[c]
constraints.intent: The whole layout must fit within the available page width. The total of all the column widths must not exceed the page width.

constraints.name: toth
constraints.intent: The total height of the table adds up the heights chosen for the rows. Set the total height equal to the sum, over all rows, of the candidate height that each row is set to.

query: We're sketching a tighter print frame where the columns together can't span more than 320 units across. If we hold the sum of all column widths to at most 320, what total height does the optimizer land on?
expected_pyomo: model.cap_total = Constraint(expr=sum(model.xCw[c] for c in model.C) <= 320)

query: Row 1 carries some dense content and we're curious about giving it more vertical room. Suppose we require row 1 to take at least the 40-unit height band rather than a short one — what does that push the total table height to?

query: We're looking at a much narrower layout where the columns combined have to stay within 300 units across. With the page squeezed that hard, what total table height comes back?
expected_pyomo: model.narrow_page = Constraint(expr=sum(model.xCw[c] for c in model.C) <= 300)

query: Row 3 came out at just 20 units tall, the same short band as row 1, even though it's holding real content. Shouldn't it get at least a 30-unit band so the text isn't cramped? What's the model seeing that makes keeping it that short worthwhile?
